title: pilosella brachiata subsp. pieniakensis
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. pieniakensis is a subspecies of the Pilosella brachiata plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, Pilosella brachiata subsp. pieniakensis has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of this plant are thought to include relief from arthritis, improved wound healing, and anti-cancer properties. Pilosella brachiata subsp. pieniakensis can be prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, and it may also be used in traditional medicine as a salve or ointment for topical applications.
